original_abstract: This article examines the operational reality of the Pattani Central Mosque to provide a counter-narrative to the prevailing discourse surrounding mosques in Southern Thailand. In recent years, these sites of worship have often been characterized as breeding grounds for Islamic radicalization and recruitment hubs for young militants opposing the Thai government. While the tragic 2004 Kru-ze Mosque incident highlighted a failure to maintain religious sites as "sanctuaries from violence," this study investigates the actual nature of contemporary Islamic teaching and mosque management. By analyzing the perspectives of key stakeholders—including Imams, preachers, and the congregation—the research explores local attitudes toward good governance, human rights, and the prevention of extremism. Ultimately, the findings suggest that the Pattani Central Mosque is defined by a balanced synthesis of moderate and conservative religious perspectives, rather than the radical or extremist tendencies frequently attributed to the region.
humanized_abstract: This paper aims to reveal the social life at the Pattani Central Mosque, a counter-balance to the dominant discourse that the mosques in Southern Thailand are involved in the spreading of extremist Islam, as well as being the hotbeds for recruiting youth to carry out armed rebellion against the Thai government. In addition, the killing of people at the Kru-ze Mosque in 2004 is widely seen as an incident where the mosque failed to remain as a place of "safety from violence" which was the original aim when it was first built. Therefore, this research will look into the teachings of Islam and the social management of the mosque and will present the views of Imams, muezzins and members of society in relation to democratic administration, human rights and measures to prevent extremism with an aim to reveal the conditions of the social life of the Pattani Central Mosque and to find out how the members of society act in accordance with moderate or extremist schools of thought. And the results show that the Pattani Central Mosque is a balance of traditional and moderate ways of religion that are far away from extremist or radical schools of thought.
